introRows.html: Each Unit, weapon, and ability in the game has a set of characteristics represented on cards. This Part explains the anatomy of Unit Cards and Weapon Profiles, what each statistic means, and how to read the data that drives every action on the battlefield.
introRows.plain: Each Unit, weapon, and ability in the game has a set of characteristics represented on cards. This Part explains the anatomy of Unit Cards and Weapon Profiles , what each statistic means, and how to read the data that drives every action on the battlefield.

rows.html: Every Unit has a Unit Card that provides all the information needed to use it in a game.
rows.plain: Every Unit has a Unit Card that provides all the information needed to use it in a game.

rows.html: 1. Unit Name
rows.plain: 1. Unit Name

rows.html: 2. Speed: The maximum distance in inches the Leading Model may traverse in a standard Move.
rows.plain: 2. Speed: The maximum distance in inches the Leading Model may traverse in a standard Move .

rows.html:
Split Values (e. g. 4"/7"): Use the second value only when the Unit is reduced to a single remaining model, or when the Unit started with a single model.
Null Value (-): The Unit cannot move or be repositioned by any means, including PLACE and involuntary movement effects.

3. Evade: A secondary defensive characteristic representing agility. Used only when a rule or condition specifically grants an Evade Roll.
A Null Value (-): The Unit cannot make Evade Rolls. An ability that explicitly grants an Evade Roll does not override this.

4. Armour: The Target Number the defender must meet or exceed on a D6 to cancel a hit.

5. Hit Points (HP): Damage a single model must sustain before it is removed.

5A. Shields (Optional): If this value is present on a Unit Card, add it to the Hit Points (HP) of the first model. The Unit is Shielded. The Unit loses its Shielded Status when the Total Damage assigned to it exceeds its Shield value or when the first model in the Unit is removed. The Shielded Status is referenced by other abilities. Losing Shielded Status does not remove any remaining Hit Points, it only ends effects that require the Unit to be Shielded.

6. Size: The model’s vertical height category, used for Line of Sight and terrain interaction.

7. Supply Profile: Links the number of remaining models in the Unit to its Current Supply Value. Update immediately when casualties reduce the model count to a lower bracket. Supply is used for Deployment, Mission Marker Control, and Disengage checks.

8. Phase Boxes: Special Abilities available during specific Phases.

9. Weapon Profiles: Each weapon is assigned to either the Assault Phase or the Combat Phase and may be used only during that phase. Each weapon profile lists:
Phase: The phase in which this weapon may be used - Assault Phase (ranged weapons) or Combat Phase (melee weapons).
[RNG] Range: Maximum effective distance. Melee weapons list E (Engagement Range). Template weapons list FT or BT.
Target: What the weapon can hit. Ground (Ground Units only), Flying (Flying Units only), or All (both Ground and Flying Units).
[RoA] Rate of Attack: Dice rolled per firing model.
Hit: Target Number required on a D6 to score a hit.
Surge Type: The Combat Tag that triggers the weapon’s Surge efficiency.
S Dice: Die type rolled to determine Surge results (D3 or D6).
[DMG] Damage: Damage inflicted per die in the Damage Pool.
Keywords: Special rules on the weapon (e. g. , PIERCE, SIDEARM).

10. Combat Tags: The Unit’s Combat Tag s.

11. Faction Tag: The Faction the Unit belongs to.

rows.html: The reverse of each Unit Card lists optional Upgrades available during Army Building.
rows.plain: The reverse of each Unit Card lists optional Upgrades available during Army Building .

rows.html: Upgrades: New abilities or weapons added to the profile.
rows.plain: Upgrades: New abilities or weapons added to the profile.

rows.html: Replacements (↑ FOR): An entry showing "↑ FOR [Weapon Name]" means the new weapon replaces the original entirely.
rows.plain: Replacements (↑ FOR): An entry showing " ↑ FOR [Weapon Name] " means the new weapon replaces the original entirely.

rows.html: Null Value (-): The Unit lacks this capability. No roll is possible.
rows.plain: Null Value (-): The Unit lacks this capability. No roll is possible.

rows.html: Army Slot: The slot type (Core, Elite, Support, Air, or Hero) required to field this Unit. Each Unit occupies a number of slots of this type equal to its starting Supply Value (Part 9. 1. 6).
rows.plain: Army Slot : The slot type (Core, Elite, Support, Air, or Hero) required to field this Unit. Each Unit occupies a number of slots of this type equal to its starting Supply Value (Part 9. 1. 6 ).

rows.html:
Flavour text describing the Unit’s role.
• Army Slot: The slot type (Core, Elite, Support, Air, or Hero) the Unit occupies. The number of occupied slots equals the unit' s starting Supply Value.
• Base diameter on which the model must be mounted on.
rows.plain: Flavour text describing the Unit’s role. • Army Slot : The slot type (Core, Elite, Support, Air, or Hero) the Unit occupies. The number of occupied slots equals the unit' s starting Supply Value . • Base diameter on which the model must be mounted on.

rows.html: No commander goes into combat alone with guns. A creep tumor in a good position, a sensor sweep in reserve, or a psionic storm held back can change a lost battle into a rout. The Tactics Cards are the strategic resources, orders, and doctrines a military leader has available to him, bought before the battle and deployed when they are most needed.
rows.plain: No commander goes into combat alone with guns. A creep tumor in a good position, a sensor sweep in reserve, or a psionic storm held back can change a lost battle into a rout. The Tactics Cards are the strategic resources, orders, and doctrines a military leader has available to him, bought before the battle and deployed when they are most needed.

rows.html: Tactical Cards are purchased with Vespene Gas during Army Building (Part 9. 1. 4).
rows.plain: Tactical Cards are purchased with Vespene Gas during Army Building (Part 9. 1. 4 ).

rows.html: Name: The name of the Tactical Card.
rows.plain: Name: The name of the Tactical Card .

rows.html: Unique Marking: If present, only one copy of this card may be included in an army.
rows.plain: Unique Marking: If present, only one copy of this card may be included in an army.

rows.html: Faction Tags: The Race Tag and any Sub-Faction Tags that determine which armies may include this card (Part 9. 1. 2).
rows.plain: Faction Tags : The Race Tag and any Sub- Faction Tags that determine which armies may include this card (Part 9. 1. 2 ).

rows.html: Resource Type and Value: The faction’s resource (CP for Terran, BM for Zerg, PE for Protoss) and the amount generated when this card is Exhausted instead of using its Special Ability.
rows.plain: Resource Type and Value: The faction’s resource ( CP for Terran , BM for Zerg , PE for Protoss ) and the amount generated when this card is Exhausted instead of using its Special Ability .

rows.html: Army Slots: The organisation slots unlocked when this card is included in the army (e. g. , 2× Core, 1× Support). These slots are in addition to those provided by the Faction Card.
rows.plain: Army Slots: The organisation slots unlocked when this card is included in the army (e. g. , 2× Core, 1× Support). These slots are in addition to those provided by the Faction Card .

rows.html: Special Abilities: One or more Active, Passive, or Reaction Abilities resolved using the standard rules (Parts 10. 1–10. 4).
rows.plain: Special Abilities : One or more Active , Passive , or Reaction Abilities resolved using the standard rules (Parts 10. 1 – 10. 4 ).

rows.html: The Faction Card is the foundation of every army. It defines who you are, what doctrine you follow, and what forces you are permitted to bring to the battlefield.
rows.plain: The Faction Card is the foundation of every army. It defines who you are, what doctrine you follow, and what forces you are permitted to bring to the battlefield.

rows.html: Every army must include exactly one Faction Card, selected during Army Building.
rows.plain: Every army must include exactly one Faction Card , selected during Army Building .

rows.html: Faction Name: The name of the Faction. This may also function as a Keyword referenced by other rules and abilities.
rows.plain: Faction Name: The name of the Faction. This may also function as a Keyword referenced by other rules and abilities.

rows.html: Faction Tags: The Race Tag and any Sub-Faction Tags that specify which Units and Tactical Cards can be included in the army (Part 9. 1. 2).
rows.plain: Faction Tags : The Race Tag and any Sub- Faction Tags that specify which Units and Tactical Cards can be included in the army (Part 9. 1. 2 ).

rows.html: Starting Army Slots: The organisation slots provided by the Faction Card itself (e. g. 3× Core, 1× Hero, 2× Support). Additional slots are unlocked by purchasing Tactical Cards.
rows.plain: Starting Army Slots: The organisation slots provided by the Faction Card itself (e. g. 3× Core, 1× Hero, 2× Support). Additional slots are unlocked by purchasing Tactical Cards .

rows.html: Resource Type and Value: The faction’s resource (CP for Terran, BM for Zerg, PE for Protoss) and the amount generated when this card is Exhausted.
rows.plain: Resource Type and Value: The faction’s resource ( CP for Terran , BM for Zerg , PE for Protoss ) and the amount generated when this card is Exhausted .

rows.html: Special Abilities: One or more Active, Passive, or Reaction Abilities resolved using the standard rules (Parts 10. 1–10. 4).
rows.plain: Special Abilities : One or more Active , Passive , or Reaction Abilities resolved using the standard rules (Parts 10. 1 – 10. 4 ).

rows.html: Every conflict has a purpose. A contested line of minerals, a crashed shuttle with vital information, or a Xel’Naga relic glowing with power buried deep beneath the surface. Mission Cards decide what the conflict is about, how long the battle will last, and what must be achieved to win. The mission determines the fight, not the other way round.
rows.plain: Every conflict has a purpose. A contested line of minerals , a crashed shuttle with vital information, or a Xel’Naga relic glowing with power buried deep beneath the surface. Mission Cards decide what the conflict is about, how long the battle will last, and what must be achieved to win. The mission determines the fight, not the other way round.

rows.html: Mission Cards are chosen during the Draft (Part 9. 2).
rows.plain: Mission Cards are chosen during the Draft (Part 9. 2 ).

rows.html: Name: The name of the Mission.
rows.plain: Name: The name of the Mission.

rows.html: Engagement Scale: The game scale this mission is designed for (Skirmish, Standard, or Grand Offensive).
rows.plain: Engagement Scale : The game scale this mission is designed for (Skirmish, Standard, or Grand Offensive).

rows.html: Starting Supply: The Supply Pool available to each player in Round 1.
rows.plain: Starting Supply: The Supply Pool available to each player in Round 1.

rows.html: Supply Escalation: The amount by which the Supply Pool increases at the start of each subsequent Round.
rows.plain: Supply Escalation: The amount by which the Supply Pool increases at the start of each subsequent Round.

rows.html: Game Length: The number of Rounds played before the game ends (usually 5).
rows.plain: Game Length: The number of Rounds played before the game ends (usually 5).

rows.html: Mission Parameters: Setup instructions, Mission Marker states, and any special conditions that apply from the start of the game.
rows.plain: Mission Parameters: Setup instructions, Mission Marker states, and any special conditions that apply from the start of the game.

rows.html: Scoring Conditions: How players earn Victory Points at the end of each Round.
rows.plain: Scoring Conditions: How players earn Victory Points at the end of each Round.

rows.html: Additional Conditions: Special rules or winning conditions unique to this mission, including any instant victory triggers.
rows.plain: Additional Conditions: Special rules or winning conditions unique to this mission, including any instant victory triggers.

rows.html: The location from which you engage in the battle is as important as what you bring into it. A narrow canyon channels the entire attack into a kill zone. Parallel zones of deployment across open ground give the victory to whoever shoots first. The Deployment Cards determine the war’s geometry, entry points, restricted zones, and the ground that must be taken.
rows.plain: The location from which you engage in the battle is as important as what you bring into it. A narrow canyon channels the entire attack into a kill zone. Parallel zones of deployment across open ground give the victory to whoever shoots first. The Deployment Cards determine the war’s geometry, entry points, restricted zones, and the ground that must be taken.

rows.html: Deployment Cards are selected during the Draft
rows.plain: Deployment Cards are selected during the Draft

rows.html: Name: The name of the Deployment layout.
rows.plain: Name: The name of the Deployment layout.

rows.html: Engagement Scale: The game scale this deployment is designed for (Skirmish, Standard, or Grand Offensive).
rows.plain: Engagement Scale : The game scale this deployment is designed for (Skirmish, Standard, or Grand Offensive).

rows.html: Battlefield Dimensions: The required table size for this deployment.
rows.plain: Battlefield Dimensions: The required table size for this deployment .

rows.html: Entry Edges: The table edges assigned to each player. Units enter the battlefield from their assigned Entry Edge when deploying from Reserves.
rows.plain: Entry Edges: The table edges assigned to each player. Units enter the battlefield from their assigned Entry Edge when deploying from Reserves .

rows.html: Zone of Influence: A restricted area extending 6" inward from each player’s Entry Edge. Enemy Units arriving from Reserves cannot end their deployment within this area. The Zone of Influence does not affect Units already on the battlefield.
rows.plain: Zone of Influence : A restricted area extending 6" inward from each player’s Entry Edge . Enemy Units arriving from Reserves cannot end their deployment within this area. The Zone of Influence does not affect Units already on the battlefield.

rows.html: Marker Coordinates: The precise measurements for positioning each numbered Mission Marker on the battlefield.
rows.plain: Marker Coordinates: The precise measurements for positioning each numbered Mission Marker on the battlefield.

rows.html: Using the Deployment Card during Setup: Once the Deployment Card is selected, follow its instructions in this order: confirm the table dimensions, assign Entry Edges to each player, set Zone of Influence Markers at the corners of each player’s Entry Edge, where applicable, then set Mission Markers at the listed coordinates. See Part 9. 3 for the full setup sequence.
rows.plain: Using the Deployment Card during Setup: Once the Deployment Card is selected, follow its instructions in this order: confirm the table dimensions, assign Entry Edges to each player, set Zone of Influence Markers at the corners of each player’s Entry Edge , where applicable, then set Mission Markers at the listed coordinates. See Part 9. 3 for the full setup sequence.
